introduction:
What is faith, really?

Most people think faith means believing in something you can't see. That's part of it, but the Lectures on Faith -- seven theological lectures from the early days of the Restoration -- teach something much bigger. Faith is the first principle of action and power. It's how God created the world. It's how prophets parted seas and moved mountains. And it's how you can access God's power in your own life.

The Lectures on Faith were prepared for the School of the Prophets in Kirtland, Ohio, in 1834-35. They were included in the original Doctrine and Covenants. They are some of the clearest, most powerful doctrinal teaching in the entire Restoration -- and most members have never read them.

This book takes each lecture and explains it in plain language. You'll learn what faith really is, what you need to know about God to trust Him, why sacrifice is essential, and what faith can produce in your life. These aren't abstract ideas. They're the foundation for everything else in the gospel -- prayer, covenants, the temple, and the covenant path.

Each lesson takes about 15-20 minutes. Read the lesson, talk it over with the AI tutor, then answer a few questions in your own words.
description: A plain-language guide to the Lectures on Faith for Latter-day Saint kids. Covers all seven lectures: faith as the principle of action and power, the knowledge of God, His character and attributes, the nature of the Godhead, the necessity of sacrifice, and the effects of faith. Makes early Restoration theology accessible to young readers.
what_youll_learn: What the Lectures on Faith are and why they matter
That faith is the first principle of action and power -- not just belief
How the knowledge of God was passed from Adam through the patriarchs
What you need to know about God's character to trust Him
Why God's attributes -- knowledge, power, justice, mercy, truth -- are essential for faith
The nature of the Godhead: Father, Son, and Holy Ghost as three separate beings
Why real faith requires the willingness to sacrifice everything
What faith produces: miracles, knowledge, righteousness, and salvation
How to grow your faith and live by it every day
expectations: 15 chapters, designed for one chapter per week (4 short lessons per week). Each lesson takes about 15-20 minutes.
prerequisites: None. Stands alone. Pairs well with Laws and Covenants for Kids and the other religion books.
tutor_instructions:
This book explains the Lectures on Faith for Latter-day Saint kids ages 7-12. The Lectures are theological, but this book makes them accessible and practical.

When explaining and when grading:
- Use plain, simple words. The Lectures on Faith use formal 19th-century language -- this book translates that into kid-friendly prose. Keep it that way.
- Help kids see that faith is active and practical, not abstract. Connect every concept to their daily lives.
- The Lectures build on each other: existence of God -> character -> attributes -> nature -> sacrifice -> effects. Help kids see the progression.
- Key teaching: faith is not blind. The Lectures spend five lectures explaining what you need to KNOW about God before you can exercise faith. Faith is grounded in knowledge.
- Key teaching: sacrifice is essential. Lecture 6 is the turning point -- without willingness to sacrifice, faith cannot reach its full power.
- When grading, accept any answer that captures the lesson's core idea in the student's own words.
- Be encouraging. The goal is to help kids understand faith deeply enough to exercise it powerfully.
